comment: GC.Collect is required to drain a collectible AssemblyLoadContext during hot reload; documented MS unloadability pattern.
Second GC.Collect after WaitForPendingFinalizers is part of the documented AssemblyLoadContext unload pattern.
GC.Collect/WaitForPendingFinalizers is the documented pattern for collectible AssemblyLoadContext unloading (see MS unloadability docs).

MockPrefabCapability deliberately mocks the deprecated runtime prefab API for tests; the [Obsolete] replacement is build-time .keprefab source generation with no runtime equivalent to call
MockPrefabCapability deliberately mocks the deprecated runtime prefab API for tests; the [Obsolete] replacement is build-time .keprefab source generation with no runtime equivalent to call

Deliberately exercises the deprecated runtime prefab API (EntityPrefab / RegisterPrefab / SpawnFromPrefab). The replacement is build-time source-generated .keprefab spawn methods, which have no runtime-API equivalent for registration, inheritance resolution, or error-handling behaviors under test; these tests guard the still-shipping deprecated API. API removal is a separate concern (out of scope for a test PR).

CLI migrate command boundary; converts failure to CommandResult.Failure (OperationCanceledException rethrown).
Per-file analysis of possibly corrupt user save files; one bad file must not abort the batch scan.
Snapshot parsing of corrupt/legacy save data; parser failure modes over arbitrary bytes are unknowable.
Binary snapshot analysis over arbitrary bytes; returns partial results on failure by design.
Per-file migration loop with continue-on-error semantics; one corrupt save must not abort the batch.
CLI plugin-install boundary; converts failure to CommandResult.Failure.
CLI plugin-list boundary; converts failure to CommandResult.Failure.
CLI plugin-search boundary (network + NuGet); converts failure to CommandResult.Failure.
CLI plugin-uninstall boundary; converts failure to CommandResult.Failure.
CLI plugin-update boundary (network + NuGet + registry); converts failure to CommandResult.Failure.
CLI command error boundary; converts failure to CommandResult.Failure with non-zero exit code.
CLI command error boundary; converts failure to CommandResult.Failure with non-zero exit code.
CLI command error boundary; converts failure to CommandResult.Failure with non-zero exit code.
Top-level CLI error boundary: converts any failure to a clean message and exit code 1 instead of a raw crash.
Optional TestBridge IPC subsystem startup; editor must run even if the bridge cannot start.
Optional scene TestBridge startup; failure only disables the diagnostic bridge.
TestBridge async dispose during scene close; teardown failure must not abort scene close.
Optional font loading with graceful fallback; third-party font loader failure modes are unknowable.
Hot-reload boundary around user game assembly rebuild/reload; must report failure, not crash the editor.
Loading user game assemblies; loader failure modes (BadImageFormat, TypeLoad, user module ctors) are open-ended.
Wraps external dotnet build process invocation; must degrade to BuildResult failure.
Activator.CreateInstance of user system types during hot reload; user constructors can throw anything.
Hot-reload configuration boundary; a broken user project must not crash the editor.
Bake pipeline over user scene geometry via third-party builder; failure must surface as a bake-failure progress report.
Try-pattern over possibly corrupt navmesh files; third-party (DotRecast) deserializer failure modes are unknowable.
Plugin Initialize() isolation boundary; a misbehaving plugin must not crash the editor.
Plugin Shutdown() isolation boundary; disable must degrade to Failed state, not crash.
Plugin install planning boundary (network + NuGet); any failure converts to InstallationPlan.Invalid.
Plugin install execution boundary; any failure converts to InstallationResult.Failed.
Plugin uninstall boundary; any failure converts to UninstallResult.Failed.
Cascade uninstall boundary; any failure converts to UninstallResult.Failed.
Plugin load isolation boundary; assembly/type/ctor failures from third-party plugins must not crash the editor.
Plugin unload isolation boundary; failure is recorded as diagnostics, not propagated.
Plugin manifest load boundary over untrusted third-party plugin content; a bad manifest must not abort discovery.
Parses untrusted/possibly malformed PE metadata; must return an Error analysis result instead of crashing.
Analyzes untrusted plugin assemblies; any failure must fail safe to an Error verification result.
Plugin-registered callback invocation; one misbehaving plugin callback must not prevent notifying the rest.
Serializes arbitrary user/plugin component types whose property getters can throw anything; a bad component must not crash the editor.
Deserializes arbitrary user/plugin component types; converter/ctor failure modes are unknowable.
Activator.CreateInstance on user/plugin types; user constructors can throw anything.
User-shader compilation boundary: preview must surface any compiler failure as an error string, never crash the editor.
Top-level demo entry point wrapping windowing/graphics init, which can surface many platform exceptions (no display, driver, GL context); recovers by reporting and exiting. Explanatory comment added.
Top-level demo entry point wrapping windowing/input init, which can surface many platform exceptions (no display, driver, GL context); recovers by reporting and exiting. Explanatory comment added.
Top-level demo entry point loading external, possibly corrupt save data; recovers by reporting and exiting. Explanatory comment retained per project precedent.
Top-level demo entry point wrapping windowing/graphics init, which can surface many platform exceptions (no display, driver, GL context); recovers by reporting and exiting. Explanatory comment added.

The (object) cast boxes the struct so the array's element type unifies to (ComponentInfo, object); removing it makes the two array elements have incompatible types and fails to compile. The upcast is required, not useless.

The (IEnumerable) cast intentionally selects the explicit non-generic IEnumerable.GetEnumerator() implementation so this test covers that code path; without the cast the generic IEnumerable<Entity> enumerator is used instead.
